evals.prompt: I want to build a workflow that receives a GitHub webhook when a PR is opened and automatically posts a Slack notification with the PR title and author. What pattern should I use and what nodes do I need?
evals.expected_output: Webhook Processing pattern. Nodes: Webhook trigger → Set (format message using $json.body fields) → Slack (post to channel). Reference the $json.body structure for GitHub webhook data.
expectations: The output identifies this as the Webhook Processing pattern
The output includes a Webhook node as the trigger
The output includes a Slack node to send the notification
The output includes a transformation step (Set node or Code node) to format the message
The output references $json.body to access the webhook payload data
The output follows the Webhook → Transform → Notify sequence
The output mentions that the Webhook node should use POST method
The output mentions that GitHub PR data arrives under $json.body fields (e.g. $json.body.pull_request)
The output does NOT set responseMode to 'responseNode' (no respondToWebhook node needed for a notification workflow)

evals.prompt: I need to build an AI chatbot that can answer customer questions and look up order status from our database. What n8n workflow pattern and nodes should I use?
evals.expected_output: AI Agent Workflow pattern. AI Agent node with OpenAI Chat Model via ai_languageModel, database tool node via ai_tool, memory via ai_memory. Trigger via Webhook or Telegram.
expectations: The output identifies this as the AI Agent Workflow pattern
The output includes the @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent node
The output connects the OpenAI Chat Model via 'ai_languageModel' connection type
The output mentions connecting tool nodes via 'ai_tool' connection type for database access
The output mentions memory (ai_memory connection) for conversation context
The output uses the correct LangChain connection type notation (ai_languageModel, not main)
The output suggests an appropriate trigger (Webhook or Telegram Trigger)
The output specifies typeVersion 1.7 for the AI Agent node
The output does NOT wire the OpenAI node via 'main' connection type

evals.prompt: My workflow needs to process 50,000 customer records from a database, transform each one, and write them to another system. It times out when I try to process them all at once. What's the right architecture?
evals.expected_output: Loop pattern with Split In Batches node. Process in chunks (e.g. 100 at a time). Wire output 0 (batch) back to Split In Batches. Output 1 (done) goes to the next step. This prevents timeouts.
expectations: The output recommends the Split In Batches node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches)
The output explains the loop wiring: batch output (main[0]) connects back to Split In Batches input
The output explains that main[1] is the 'done' output that continues after all batches
The output suggests a batch size (10, 50, 100, or similar)
The output explains this prevents timeouts by processing in smaller chunks
The output does NOT suggest processing all 50,000 records in a single Code node
The output describes the loop pattern correctly as a cycle, not a linear sequence
The output explicitly shows or states that the batch output (main[0]) loops BACK to Split In Batches input
The output does NOT suggest processing 50,000 records in a single Code node as a valid alternative

evals.prompt: I'm setting up a daily report workflow that fetches analytics data and emails it to my team. What should I always include to make it production-ready?
evals.expected_output: Schedule Trigger, fetch data, Code/Set to format, Email output. Plus: Error Trigger node → Slack/email notification for failures. Workflow settings: timezone, saveExecutionProgress:true, executionTimeout for long-running fetches.
expectations: The output includes a Schedule Trigger as the starting node
The output recommends adding an Error Trigger node for error handling
The output suggests connecting the Error Trigger to a notification (Slack, email, etc.)
The output mentions relevant workflow settings (timezone for the schedule trigger)
The output mentions saveExecutionProgress or executionTimeout as useful settings
The output follows the scheduled tasks pattern: Schedule → Fetch → Process → Deliver
The output emphasizes error handling as essential for production workflows
The output indicates the Error Trigger belongs in a SEPARATE workflow (not wired inside the same workflow)
The output includes setting the 'errorWorkflow' in the workflow settings, pointing to the error handler workflow ID

evals.prompt: I have two separate API calls that each return data I need to combine into a single output. They're independent and could run at the same time. What's the best pattern?
evals.expected_output: Parallel Processing with Merge node. Wire trigger → both API nodes simultaneously (split). Both connect to Merge node — one on input index 0, other on input index 1. Merge mode: combine by position or merge by fields.
expectations: The output recommends the Merge node to combine the two data streams
The output shows or describes a parallel branch structure (trigger connects to both API nodes)
The output explains that Merge requires two inputs wired to input index 0 and index 1
The output uses the correct 'index': 0 and 'index': 1 distinction for Merge inputs
The output suggests a merge mode (combine, mergeByPosition, or append)
The output does NOT suggest running the two API calls sequentially when they're independent
The output does NOT wire both API outputs to the same input index of Merge
The output shows the trigger connecting to BOTH API nodes (not API node 1 → API node 2 sequentially)
The output shows API node 1 connecting to Merge input index 0 and API node 2 to input index 1 (distinct indices)
